---
title: "Extended Methods & Materials"
author: "Author Name"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    smooth-scroll: true
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
---

# Introduction

Welcome to the extended methods document for our poster presentation. Because scientific posters are best designed to focus on visual results and overarching conclusions, we have offloaded the dense methodological details, pipeline parameters, and full cohort statistics to this webpage.

# Study Cohorts and Demographics

Provide a detailed breakdown of the cohorts here. This is a great place to include large tables that would otherwise clutter a physical poster.

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Example R code block to generate a demographic table
# library(gtsummary)
# data %>% tbl_summary(by = group)
```

## Inclusion & Exclusion Criteria
* List inclusion criteria here.
* List exclusion criteria here.

# Imaging Acquisition & Processing

Describe the MRI scanner settings, sequences, and quality control steps.

## Freesurfer Pipeline
We utilized FreeSurfer v7.3.2 to extract cortical thickness and subcortical volumes. Specifically, the following flags were applied:
`recon-all -all -s {subject_id} -qcache -measure thickness -fwhm 15`

## Quality Control (QC)
All scans were visually inspected using the ENIGMA QC protocols. Scans with excessive motion artifacts or failed surface segmentations were excluded.

# Statistical Analysis

## Vertex-wise GLMs
Vertex-wise general linear models (GLMs) were conducted using `BrainStat`. The primary model tested for main effects of diagnosis and interactions with aggression.
* **Equation:** `Thickness ~ Diagnosis * Aggression + Age + Sex + Site`
* **Thresholding:** Random Field Theory (RFT) cluster-level correction (p < 0.05).

## Subcortical Volumes
Volumetric analysis was performed using standard linear regression, correcting for Intracranial Volume (ICV).

# Genetics & Transcriptomics

If your poster includes genetic data, detail the GWAS data sources, PRS calculations, or gene expression datasets (e.g., AHBA) used for enrichment analysis.

---

*This document is formatted using Quarto. You can publish this directly to Quarto Pub (`quarto publish quarto-pub`) or host it on GitHub Pages, and link the URL to the QR code on your poster.*
